zh.Minster Lovel, Cornbury, Oxfordshire, En: 英國奧可福爾可恩波利
zh.Shepton Montague, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特謝普頓 蒙塔古
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Montague於大約1155年出生在英國薩默塞特謝普頓 蒙塔古時，她的父親Richard Montagu39歲，母親Alice de Basset27歲。她與William Lovel結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Minster Lovel, Cornbury, Oxfordshire, En: 英国奧可福爾可恩波利
zh-hans.Shepton Montague, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特謝普頓 蒙塔古
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Montague于大约1155年出生在英国薩默塞特謝普頓 蒙塔古时，她的父亲Richard Montagu39岁，母亲Alice de Basset27岁。她与William Lovel结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Montague
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either Montaigu-le-bois (Manche) or Montaigu (Manche, Aisne), named with Old French mont ‘hill’ + agu ‘pointed’ (from Latin acutus). Drogo de Montagud was a companion of William the Conqueror and founder of the Montagu(e) family, among whom the personal name Drogo was revived in the 19th century. Another family of the name Monatgu have held the titles of Duke of Montagu, Earl of Sandwich, Earl and Duke of Manchester, Earl of Halifax, and Earl of Beaulieu, and.
Irish: adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Taidhg (see McTigue ).

variants.name: McTigue
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Taidhg, ‘son of Tadhg’, a personal name originally meaning ‘poet’ or ‘philosopher’. Compare McCaig .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Montague was born about 1155, in Shepton Montague, Somerset, England, her father, Richard Montagu, was 39 and her mother, Alice de Basset, was 27. She married William Lovel. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Montague est née environ 1155, à Shepton Montague, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Richard Montagu, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alice de Basset, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé William Lovel. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Montague um 1155 in Shepton Montague, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Montagu 39 und ihre Mutter Alice de Basset 27. Sie hat William Lovel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Montague è nata il circa 1155 nel Shepton Montague, Somerset, England, sua padre, Richard Montagu, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Alice de Basset, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato William Lovel. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.Shepton Montague, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトシェプトン モンタグー
ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Montagueが約1155年にイギリスソメルセトシェプトン モンタグーで生まれた時，父Richard Montaguは39歳，母Alice de Bassetは27歳であった。William Lovelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Montague(이)가 대략 1155년 영국 서머세트 셰프턴 몽아구에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Montagu(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Basset(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 William Lovel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

mn.Shepton Montague, Somerset, England: Shepton Montague, Bro an Hañv, Английн

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Montague nasceu aproximadamente 1155, em Shepton Montague, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Montagu, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Basset, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com William Lovel. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Montague родилась около 1155 г. в местности шептон монтагу, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Montagu было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Basset, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Lovel. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Montague nació aproximadamente 1155, en Shepton Montague, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Montagu, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Alice de Basset, tenía 27 años. Se casó con William Lovel. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
